_comment_nist_fields: Fields below follow IR 8278Ar1 Section 3.2 OLIR template
focal_document_element_description: Installing equipment, machines, wiring, or programs to meet specifications.
reference_document_element_description: Skill in containerization and orchestration using Docker and Kubernetes, including building container images, writing Kubernetes manifests, managing deployments, and troubleshooting container runtime issues.
comments: Functional overlap: installing/deploying software systems. WIDAI covers software deployment (TF-S-009 containerization, TF-S-008 CI/CD) rather than physical installation. The functional outcome — setting up systems to specification — intersects.
_comment_widai_extensions: Fields below are WIDAI extensions per ADR-014

primary_method.name: Cross-Encoder STS
primary_method.description: Semantic Textual Similarity via cross-encoder regression trained on STS-Benchmark. Both texts processed jointly through transformer; outputs calibrated 0-1 similarity score.
primary_method.significance: Weak similarity (nist band: much more dissimilar than similar). The low STS score indicates a narrow intersection. The conceptual overlap exists but is limited — most of each description's semantic content is non-overlapping.

bertscore.description: Token-level contextual embedding matching. Precision = FDE coverage of KSA tokens. Recall = KSA coverage by FDE tokens. F1 = harmonic mean.
bertscore.significance: F1 of 0.8379 indicates lower token-level semantic alignment. Precision exceeds Recall, indicating that the FDE's semantic content is more tightly aligned with the KSA than the reverse — the KSA contains concepts or specificity beyond what the FDE covers. For an Intersects relationship, the P/R balance indicates which direction the overlap tilts — whether the FDE captures more of the KSA's content or vice versa.

nli_cross_encoder.description: Natural Language Inference. Classifies text pair as entailment, neutral, or contradiction. Indicates directional implication strength.
nli_cross_encoder.significance: Contradiction-dominant (54.00%) — the model detects tension between the descriptions. A strong contradiction signal is unusual for a mapped pair and may indicate the descriptions address the same domain from opposing perspectives, or that the NLI model is reacting to scope/specificity differences rather than actual semantic conflict. This warrants attention during human review.

biencoder_cosine.description: Bi-encoder sentence embeddings with cosine similarity. Each text encoded independently; measures vector-space proximity.
biencoder_cosine.significance: The bi-encoder score (0.2170) is close to the STS cross-encoder (0.2411). The small gap (0.02) suggests the semantic relationship is largely capturable through independent encoding — the descriptions share enough surface-level semantic features that cross-attention adds only marginal signal.

jaccard_token.description: Token-level Jaccard similarity (intersection/union of lowercased word tokens). Purely lexical baseline, hand-traceable.
jaccard_token.significance: Minimal lexical overlap (0.00% of tokens shared). Low lexical overlap combined with low STS indicates both surface vocabulary and underlying semantics diverge between these descriptions.

signals.detail: NLI contradiction 54.0% suggests semantic tension between the FDE and matched KSA. The competency domains may be adjacent but not functionally aligned.
